Package org.eventb.internal.core.ast

Class Specialization

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.Specialization
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cloneable, ISpecialization
public class Specialization extends Object implements ISpecialization
Common implementation for specializations. To ensure compatibility of the type and identifier substitution, we check that no substitution is entered twice and we also remember, for each identifier substitution the list of given types that must not change afterwards. A type substitution is also doubled as an identifier substitution.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    canPut(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
    Checks whether the proposed free-identifier substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition of ISpecialization.put(FreeIdentifier, Expression) is satisfied.
    boolean
    canPut(GivenType type, Expression value)
    Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for ISpecialization.put(GivenType, Expression) is satisfied.
    boolean
    canPut(GivenType type, Type value)
    Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for ISpecialization.put(GivenType, Type) is satisfied.

  • Method Details

    • clone

      public ISpecialization clone()
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Returns a deep copy of this specialization. This method is useful to protect a specialization from side-effects when applying it to a type environment or a mathematical formula.
      Specified by:
      clone in interface ISpecialization
      Returns:
      a deep copy of this specialization

    • getSourceTypenv

      public ITypeEnvironmentBuilder getSourceTypenv()

    • getFactory

      public FormulaFactory getFactory()
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Returns the formula factory with which this specialization was created.
      Specified by:
      getFactory in interface ISpecialization
      Returns:
      the formula factory of this specialization

    • getDestinationTypenv

      public ITypeEnvironmentBuilder getDestinationTypenv()

    • getFormulaRewriter

      public ITypeCheckingRewriter getFormulaRewriter()

    • canPut

      public boolean canPut(GivenType type, Type value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for ISpecialization.put(GivenType, Type) is satisfied. The value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance. This method does not modify the specialization instance.
      Specified by:
      canPut in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - replacement for the given type
      Returns:
      true if the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions, false otherwise.

    • put

      public void put(GivenType type, Type value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Adds a new type substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. The added substitution must be compatible with already registered substitutions (for both given types and free identifiers). The value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Specified by:
      put in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - replacement for the given type
      See Also:
      • Type.getFactory()
      • ISpecialization.canPut(GivenType, Type)

    • get

      public Type get(GivenType key)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Returns the type to be substituted for the given given type.
      Specified by:
      get in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      key - some given type
      Returns:
      the type to be substituted for the given given type or null if no substitution has been defined for the given given type

    • canPut

      public boolean canPut(GivenType type, Expression value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for ISpecialization.put(GivenType, Expression) is satisfied. The value must by a typed type expression and must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance. This method does not modify the specialization instance.
      Specified by:
      canPut in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - typed type expression replacement for the given type
      Returns:
      true if the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions, false otherwise.

    • put

      public void put(GivenType type, Expression value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Adds a new type substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. The value must be a type-checked type expression. The added substitution must be compatible with already registered substitutions (for both given types and free identifiers). The value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Specified by:
      put in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - typed type expression replacement for the given type
      See Also:
      • Type.getFactory()
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()
      • Expression.isATypeExpression()
      • ISpecialization.canPut(GivenType, Expression)

    • canPut

      public boolean canPut(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Checks whether the proposed free-identifier substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition of ISpecialization.put(FreeIdentifier, Expression) is satisfied. Both the type and the value must be type-checked. The value expression must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance. This method does not modify the specialization instance.
      Specified by:
      canPut in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      ident - a typed identifier to substitute
      value - a typed expression to substitute for the given identifier
      Returns:
      true if the proposed free identifier substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions. Return false otherwise.

    • put

      public void put(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Adds a new free identifier substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. Both parameters must be type-checked. The given identifier must not denote a type. The added substitution must be compatible with already registered substitutions (for both given types and free identifiers). The given expression needs not be well-formed. In case the expression contains bound identifiers, it is up to the caller to manage that they do not escape their scope. The given expression must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Specified by:
      put in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      ident - a typed identifier to substitute
      value - a typed expression to substitute for the given identifier
      See Also:
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()
      • Formula.getFactory()
      • ISpecialization.canPut(FreeIdentifier, Expression)

    • specialize

      public Type specialize(Type type)

      public void prepare(Type type)

      public ITypeEnvironmentBuilder specialize(TypeEnvironment typenv)

      public void prepare(TypeEnvironment typenv)

      public <T extends Formula<T>> void prepare(Formula<T> formula)

    • get

      public Expression get(FreeIdentifier ident)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Returns the expression to be substituted for the given identifier.
      Specified by:
      get in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      ident - a typed identifier
      Returns:
      the expression to be substituted for the given identifier or null if no substitution has been defined for the given identifier

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • put

      public boolean put(PredicateVariable predVar, Predicate value)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Adds a new predicate variable substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. The given predicate value must be typed, but needs not be well-formed. In case the predicate value contains bound identifiers, it is up to the caller to manage that they do not escape their scope. The given predicate value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Specified by:
      put in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      predVar - a predicate variable to substitute
      value - a typed predicate to substitute for the given predicate variable.
      Returns:
      true if the proposed predicate variable substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions, and this specialization instance has been changed accordingly. Return false otherwise and this specialization instance is unchanged.
      See Also:
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()
      • Formula.getFactory()

    • get

      public Predicate get(PredicateVariable predVar)
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Returns the predicate to be substituted for the given predicate variable.
      Specified by:
      get in interface ISpecialization
      Parameters:
      predVar - a predicate variable.
      Returns:
      the predicate to be substituted for the given predicate variable or null if no substitution has been defined for the given predicate variable

      public GivenType[] getTypes()
      Description copied from interface: ISpecialization
      Returns the set of given types to be substituted.
      Specified by:
      getTypes in interface ISpecialization
      Returns:
      the set of given types to be substituted

      Returns the set of predicate variables to be substituted.

      getPredicateVariables in interface ISpecialization
      Returns:
